skill.name: gate-exchange-spot
skill.description: Guides Gate spot trading workflows, including account checks, market data review, order drafts, confirmed order placement, cancellations, amendments, and fill verification.
skill.summary: Manage Gate spot trading workflows with confirmation gates and account checks.
skill.author: gate

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution and weak-cryptography alerts are false positives from Markdown code spans, file names, tool names, and scenario text. The real risk is legitimate but high-impact: the skill can guide authenticated Gate spot trading actions and documents API-key based integration tests. No evidence found of malware, prompt injection, credential exfiltration, or hidden executable code.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Spot Trading Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill intentionally guides Gate spot trading operations, including create_spot_order, cancel_spot_order, amend_spot_order, and balance-related calls. This can move or affect real assets when connected to an account, although the files require order drafts and immediate user confirmation before placement.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented tool map and workflow clearly include write-capable spot trading operations. Confidence is very high because the same files also describe confirmation controls, so this is legitimate high-impact functionality rather than malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Write-Enabled API Test Instructions
medium_findings.description: The README documents Gate API credential environment variables and a write-enabled real integration test mode. This is not secret exfiltration, but users could expose credentials or place real test trades if they run write mode carelessly.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment variables and write-mode flag are explicit in the README. Confidence is high that this is a real operational risk, but the file does not contain code that reads or transmits secrets.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged many backtick-delimited Markdown snippets as Ruby shell execution. Reviewed examples are inline tool names, file names, command examples, or fenced documentation, not executable skill code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited files are Markdown documentation and the flagged backticks delimit text. No Ruby files, shell interpolation, or executable command-dispatch logic were found in the reviewed skill files.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography alerts occur in documentation and scenario lines that describe trading workflows, Markdown files, or order examples. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, custom cryptography, or cryptographic API usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found normal Markdown and trading scenario prose at the flagged locations. Confidence is high because there is no executable cryptographic implementation in the four scanned files.

dangerous_patterns.title: Trading Tool Calls Can Affect Real Assets
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill maps user requests to Gate spot tools for order placement, cancellation, amendment, fill lookup, and account checks. These capabilities require marketplace warnings because connected accounts may perform real trades.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The tool names and case map directly show account and order-management capabilities. Confidence is high because the risk comes from intended functionality, not inferred hidden code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Real Credential Variables In Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: The README shows GATE_API_KEY and GATE_API_SECRET for real API integration tests. The documented variables are expected for exchange access, but they increase the impact of misuse or unsafe test execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The credential variable names are explicit at the cited lines. Confidence is high that this is credential-related documentation, while review found no code that captures or exfiltrates the values.

content.value_statement: Gate spot trading requires careful balance checks, price validation, and order confirmation. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through account queries, draft orders, and confirmed trading actions.
actual_capabilities: Checks Gate spot account balances and available funds before proposed trades.
Builds market and limit order drafts with pair, side, amount, price, and risk notes.
Routes confirmed buy, sell, cancel, and amend requests to the documented Gate spot tools.
Reviews open orders, fills, holdings, fees, trading rules, tickers, order books, and candles.
Handles conditional spot workflows by calculating target prices and drafting limit orders.
Explains unsupported TP/SL requests and proposes manual limit-order alternatives.
limitations: It requires configured Gate trading tools and valid account permissions.
It can affect real assets when the user confirms write actions.
It does not provide investment advice or guarantee execution price.
It does not support native take-profit or stop-loss trigger orders.

use_cases.title: Check Readiness Before Buying
use_cases.description: Verify tradability, balance, minimum order size, and estimated cost before drafting a Gate spot buy order.

use_cases.title: Manage Open Spot Orders
use_cases.description: List open orders, cancel unfilled orders, amend limit prices, and confirm the resulting balance state.

use_cases.title: Reconcile Fills And Holdings
use_cases.description: Compare recent trade fills with current balances to confirm received amounts and account value.

prompt_templates.title: Check My Balance
prompt_templates.prompt: Check my Gate spot balance for USDT and BTC. Show available amounts and total value in USDT.

prompt_templates.title: Draft A Buy Order
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to buy 100 USDT of BTC on Gate spot. Check my balance and prepare an order draft only.

prompt_templates.title: Amend An Open Order
prompt_templates.prompt: Find my open BTC_USDT buy orders and prepare an amendment draft to raise one limit price by 1 percent.

prompt_templates.title: Run A Conditional Swap
prompt_templates.prompt: If my DOGE position is worth at least 10 USDT, prepare a per-leg draft to swap DOGE into BTC.

output_examples.input: Buy 100 USDT of BTC if my balance is enough.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks USDT balance, verifies BTC_USDT rules, and returns an order draft. It waits for explicit confirmation before placing any order.

output_examples.input: Cancel all open ETH orders and show my balance.
output_examples.output: The assistant cancels matching open spot orders, then refreshes balances and reports canceled orders plus updated available funds.
The assistant reviews recent BTC_USDT trades, compares them with current BTC holdings, and summarizes fill quantity, price, fees, and balance.

best_practices: Use read-only balance and market checks before drafting any trade.
Review pair, side, amount, price, and slippage notes before confirming orders.
Use small test amounts when validating real API or write-enabled workflows.
anti_patterns: Do not confirm an order until the draft matches your intended pair and amount.
Do not use old confirmations after changing price, symbol, side, or order type.
Do not grant write-enabled API keys unless real trading is intended.

faq.question: Can this skill place real Gate spot orders?
faq.answer: Yes. It can route confirmed order placement through Gate spot tools when write permissions are available.

faq.question: Does it place orders automatically?
faq.answer: It is designed to present an order draft first and require explicit confirmation before each order.

faq.question: Can it manage open orders?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers listing, canceling, and amending spot orders when the required Gate tools are configured.

faq.question: Does it support stop-loss or take-profit triggers?
faq.answer: No. The skill states that native TP/SL trigger orders are unsupported and suggests manual limit alternatives.

faq.question: Does it need API keys?
faq.answer: Real integration testing and account operations require Gate credentials with suitable permissions.

faq.question: Is it financial advice?
faq.answer: No. It helps execute user-directed workflows and does not provide investment recommendations.
